quiz_items.question: When is a scanning safeguard required for laser light shows?
options: For any scanning effect exposing audience to radiation >5 mW peak power
quiz_items.explanation: Any scanning effect that may expose audience to scanned radiation >5 mW peak power requires a scanning safeguard because stopping/slowing could produce Class IIIb/IV levels and acute injury risk.

quiz_items.question: Failure to provide timely notifications of show schedules is a violation of variance conditions and may result in variance revocation.
quiz_items.explanation: True. Repeated failures to report, inadequate reporting, or failure to provide timely notifications are violations and may lead to variance amendment requirements or revocation.
